Their enemies had carried the Jewish people captive from their own land. To make matters worse, they insulted them and demanded they provide entertainment and songs. This was very cruel; also disrespectful, because they would only accept songs praising Zion. We should not associate with mockers. They do not ask, How can we sing when we are so sad? but, It is the Lord's song, therefore we dare not sing it among idolaters.

What we love, we love to think about. Those who rejoice in God make Jerusalem their joy for his sake. They firmly resolved to maintain this affection. When suffering, we should remember with godly sorrow our lost blessings and the sins by which we lost them. If worldly advantages ever influence a profession, the worst calamity has befallen that person. Let us not avenge ourselves; we will leave it to Him who has said, Vengeance is mine. Those who rejoice at calamities, especially at the calamities of Jerusalem, shall not go unpunished. We cannot pray for promised success to the church of God without looking to, though we do not utter a prayer for, the ruin of her enemies. But let us remember that it is only through his grace and completed salvation that we have any hope of being brought home to the heavenly Jerusalem.
